questions.question: Explain why coordinate geometry is called analytical geometry.
questions.answer: Because geometric problems are solved using algebraic methods.

questions.question: Justify that the origin does not belong to any quadrant.
questions.answer: It lies on both axes and quadrants exclude axes.

questions.question: Explain how signs of coordinates determine quadrants.
questions.answer: Each quadrant has a unique sign combination of x and y.

questions.question: What happens to coordinates when a point moves diagonally upward?
questions.answer: Both x and y coordinates increase.

questions.question: What happens to coordinates when a point moves diagonally downward?
questions.answer: Both x and y coordinates decrease.

questions.question: Can a point have fractional coordinates? Explain.
questions.answer: Yes, coordinates can be any real numbers.

questions.question: Explain why ordered pairs are written in brackets.
questions.answer: To distinguish x and y positions clearly.

questions.question: Explain the importance of scale while plotting points.
questions.answer: Scale ensures correct distances and accuracy.

questions.question: Can two different points lie on same vertical line?
questions.answer: Yes, they can have same x-coordinate.

questions.question: Can two different points lie on same horizontal line?
questions.answer: Yes, they can have same y-coordinate.

questions.question: Explain the role of coordinate geometry in maps.
questions.answer: It helps locate places precisely using coordinates.

questions.question: Why are axes perpendicular to each other?
questions.answer: To form a reference frame for horizontal and vertical directions.

questions.question: Is it possible for a point to lie equally distant from both axes?
questions.answer: Yes, when |x| = |y|.

questions.question: Explain how coordinate geometry links algebra and geometry.
questions.answer: Coordinates convert geometric shapes into algebraic expressions.

questions.question: Why is the Cartesian plane two-dimensional?
questions.answer: Because it uses two perpendicular axes.

questions.question: Explain the significance of negative coordinates.
questions.answer: They indicate direction opposite to positive axes.

questions.question: Can the same point be represented by two different ordered pairs?
questions.answer: No, each point has a unique ordered pair.

questions.question: Explain why axes divide the plane symmetrically.
questions.answer: They create equal regions around origin.

questions.question: Is (−x, −y) the reflection of (x, y)? Explain.
questions.answer: Yes, it is reflection through the origin.

questions.question: Explain the concept of reference point in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: The origin acts as reference for measuring positions.

questions.question: Why are coordinates always written as (x, y) and not (y, x)?
questions.answer: Because order defines direction and position.

questions.question: What error occurs if axes are not perpendicular?
questions.answer: Distances and positions become inaccurate.

questions.question: Explain how coordinate geometry helps in graphing.
questions.answer: It provides exact numerical positions for plotting.

questions.question: Why is scale selection important in plotting large values?
questions.answer: To fit the graph accurately on paper.

questions.question: Summarize the importance of coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: It connects algebra with geometry for precise analysis.
